title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: One call gets interactive brain: Click drag rotate, scroll zoom, right-click pan. Hover region see name.

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: pan_camera() snaps camera standard anatomical views: full set presets: left lateral, right lateral left medial, right medial left superior, right superior left inferior, right inferior left anterior, right anterior left posterior, right posterior anything else, pass explicit coordinates:
code: ggseg3d() |>   pan_camera("left lateral") ggseg3d() |>   pan_camera(list(eye = list(x = -200, y = 100, z = 100)))

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: core workflow: bring data frame region (label) column matches atlas, map variable colour_by. Add text_by surface values hover tooltip: Hover coloured regions — tooltip now shows region’s p-value.
code: some_data <- tibble(   region = c("precentral", "postcentral", "insula", "superior parietal"),   p = c(0.01, 0.04, 0.2, 0.5) )  ggseg3d(.data = some_data, atlas = dk(), colour_by = "p", text_by = "p") |>   pan_camera("right lateral")

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: unnamed vector defines gradient endpoints: named vector pins colours specific breakpoints, lets scale extend beyond data range:
code: ggseg3d(   .data = some_data,   atlas = dk(),   colour_by = "p",   palette = c("forestgreen", "white", "firebrick") ) ggseg3d(   .data = some_data,   atlas = dk(),   colour_by = "p",   text_by = "p",   palette = c("forestgreen" = 0, "white" = .05, "firebrick" = 1) )

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: White works journals. Dark backgrounds work slides:

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: Experimental. Edge rendering works htmlwidget viewer. rgl support (ggsegray) still unreliable across platforms. set_edges() draws outlines along region boundaries:

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: default, edges appear wherever adjacent vertices different colours. edge_by lets group edges different column — useful want boundaries lobes individual regions within lobe:
code: lobe_data <- tibble(   region = c("precentral", "postcentral", "insula", "superior parietal"),   p = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.3, 0.5),   lobe = c("frontal", "parietal", "insula", "parietal") )  ggseg3d(.data = lobe_data, atlas = dk(), colour_by = "p", edge_by = "lobe") |>   set_edges("white", width = 1) |>   set_background("black")

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: aseg atlas covers structures beneath cortex. Fade unmatched regions na_alpha wrap everything glass brain: glass brain just translucent cortical surface — enough anatomy orient viewer without hiding structures underneath.
code: subcort_data <- tibble(   region = c("Thalamus", "Caudate", "Hippocampus"),   p = c(0.2, 0.5, 0.8) )  ggseg3d(   .data = subcort_data,   atlas = aseg(),   colour_by = "p",   text_by = "p",   na_alpha = .5 ) |>   add_glassbrain()

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: tracula atlas renders white matter pathways tube meshes. near-invisible glass brain keeps things oriented:

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: Data mapping works way cortical subcortical atlases — region column matches tract names:
code: tract_data <- tibble(   region = c("arcuate fasciculus", "corticospinal tract",              "cingulum bundle", "uncinate fasciculus"),   fa = c(0.45, 0.55, 0.40, 0.35) )  ggseg3d(   .data = tract_data,   atlas = tracula(),   colour_by = "fa",   text_by = "fa" ) |>   add_glassbrain(opacity = 0.1)

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: tract_color = "orientation" encodes fibre direction RGB — red left-right, green anterior-posterior, blue superior-inferior. convention used DTI colour maps:

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: tube_radius controls thickness, tube_segments controls smoothness. Higher segments look better render slower:

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: snapshot_brain() renders widget PNG using headless browser (Chrome Chromium required):

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: print, crank resolution: width/height — output dimensions pixels zoom — resolution multiplier (2-3 print) delay — seconds wait renderer finish
code: ggseg3d(.data = some_data, atlas = dk(), colour_by = "p") |>   pan_camera("left lateral") |>   set_background("white") |>   snapshot_brain(     "figure1_brain.png",     width = 1200,     height = 1000,     zoom = 3,     delay = 2   )

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: Snapshot view separately, stitch together magick:
code: library(magick)  base_plot <- ggseg3d(.data = some_data, atlas = dk(), colour_by = "p") |>   set_background("white")  base_plot |>   pan_camera("left lateral") |>   snapshot_brain("left_lat.png") base_plot |>   pan_camera("left medial") |>   snapshot_brain("left_med.png") base_plot |>   pan_camera("right lateral") |>   snapshot_brain("right_lat.png") base_plot |>   pan_camera("right medial") |>   snapshot_brain("right_med.png")  panels <- image_read(c(   "left_lat.png",   "left_med.png",   "right_lat.png",   "right_med.png" )) combined <- image_montage(panels, geometry = "600x500", tile = "2x2") image_write(combined, "figure1_all_views.png") ## agg_png  ##       2 ## [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: Lighting adds depth shifts colours. set_flat_shading() turns lighting every vertex renders exact assigned colour — useful atlas illustrations mask extraction:

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: Perspective projection makes distant regions look smaller. Orthographic projection removes depth cue, every region appears scale regardless distance camera:

title: ggseg3d walkthrough
text: ggseg_atlas objects work ggseg (2D polygons) ggseg3d (3D meshes). One atlas, two views:

title: Introduction to ggseg3d
text: arguments, ggseg3d() plots Desikan-Killiany atlas. output htmlwidget: click drag rotate, scroll zoom, hover see region names.

title: Introduction to ggseg3d
text: Match data atlas region name, point colour_by variable care : text_by = "p" adds p-value hover tooltip can inspect individual regions without separate table.
code: library(dplyr)  some_data <- tibble(   region = c("precentral", "postcentral", "insula", "superior parietal"),   p = c(0.01, 0.04, 0.2, 0.5) )  ggseg3d(.data = some_data, atlas = dk(), colour_by = "p", text_by = "p") |>   pan_camera("right lateral")

title: Introduction to ggseg3d
text: Cortical surfaces half story. aseg atlas covers subcortical structures, add_glassbrain() wraps translucent cortex anatomical context:
code: subcort_data <- tibble(   region = c("Thalamus", "Caudate", "Hippocampus"),   p = c(0.2, 0.5, 0.8) )  ggseg3d(.data = subcort_data, atlas = aseg(), colour_by = "p", na_alpha = .5) |>   add_glassbrain()

title: Introduction to ggseg3d
text: pan_camera() sets viewing angle. set_background() changes canvas colour — handy dark-themed slides: Camera presets cover standard anatomical views: "left lateral", "right medial", "left superior", . anything else, pass list eye coordinates.

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: rgl provides 3D scene, rayshader provides ray tracer. optional dependencies — ggseg3d checks runtime.

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: ggsegray() mirrors ggseg3d() API atlas, hemisphere, colour mapping. difference: instead htmlwidget, get rgl window. Camera, background, glass brain overlays — comes pipe functions already know widget side. rgl window interactive — rotate mouse, zoom scroll wheel. camera presets match ones pan_camera(): “left lateral”, “right medial”, “left superior”, .
code: ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-first.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Data mapping works identically ggseg3d(). Provide data frame region column, point colour_by numeric categorical variable, optionally set custom palette:
code: ggsegray(   .data = some_data,   atlas = dk(),   colour_by = "p",   hemisphere = "left",   palette = c("forestgreen" = 0, "white" = 0.05, "firebrick" = 1) ) |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-data.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: reaching ray tracer, grab fast screenshot rgl::snapshot3d(): ray tracing, wait. angle lighting look right, switch render_highquality() final version.
code: p <- ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("my_brain.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Build scene ggsegray() pipe functions, call render_highquality(): Higher samples means less noise longer render times. drafts, 64-128 fine. final figures, 256-512 produces clean results.
code: ggsegray(   .data = some_data,   atlas = dk(),   colour_by = "p",   hemisphere = "left" ) |>   pan_camera("left lateral") |>   set_background("white")  rayshader::render_highquality(   filename = "raytrace.png",   samples = 64,   width = 600,   height = 450 ) rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Camera presets map positions Three.js viewer. Pipe pan_camera() ggsegray(): custom viewpoint, pass numeric vector c(x, y, z): rgl window open, rgl::view3d() rgl::observer3d() let fine-tune interactively rendering.
code: ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("lateral.png") rgl::close3d() ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left medial")  rgl::snapshot3d("medial.png") rgl::close3d() ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "right") |>   pan_camera("right superior")  rgl::snapshot3d("superior.png") rgl::close3d() ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera(c(-300, 100, 150))  rgl::snapshot3d("custom_angle.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Rayshader controls lighting render_highquality(). light_direction light_altitude parameters set angle elevation light source: richer lighting setups — multiple light sources, coloured lights, area lights — pass custom scene elements:
code: ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rayshader::render_highquality(   filename = "lighting.png",   samples = 64,   light_direction = 90,   light_altitude = 30 ) rgl::close3d() ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rayshader::render_highquality(   filename = "custom_light.png",   samples = 64,   width = 600,   height = 450,   light = FALSE,   scene_elements = rayrender::sphere(     x = -300, y = 300, z = 200,     radius = 50,     material = rayrender::light(intensity = 80, color = "white")   ),   interactive = FALSE ) rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: White backgrounds work journals. posters slides, dark backgrounds make brain pop:
code: ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral") |>   set_background("black")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-dark.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Subcortical structures float space without context. translucent glass brain fixes : Lower opacity values make glass brain see-. subcortical atlases, 0.1-0.2 gives enough context without obscuring structures underneath.
code: ggsegray(atlas = aseg()) |>   add_glassbrain(colour = "#CCCCCC", opacity = 0.15) |>   pan_camera("right lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-glassbrain.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: tracula atlas works ggsegray() . Tracts render tube meshes: Direction-based RGB colouring tract_color = "orientation":
code: ggsegray(atlas = tracula()) |>   add_glassbrain(opacity = 0.1) |>   pan_camera("right lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-tracula.png") rgl::close3d() ggsegray(atlas = tracula(), tract_color = "orientation") |>   add_glassbrain(opacity = 0.1) |>   pan_camera("left lateral") |>   set_background("black")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-tracula-orient.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: ggsegray() accepts material argument — named list rgl material properties (see ?rgl::material3d full list). gives control lighting, reflection, shading without ggseg3d needing wrap every option. Glossy highlights — set specular = "white" (default "black" matte): Flat colours (lighting) — set lit = FALSE disable shading. Every vertex renders exact assigned colour. Essential mask extraction shadows contaminate output: Wireframe — set front = "lines" wireframe view mesh geometry: property accepted rgl::material3d() can go material list — ambient, emission, smooth, alpha, lwd, .
code: ggsegray(   atlas = dk(),   hemisphere = "left",   material = list(specular = "white", shininess = 100) ) |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-glossy.png") rgl::close3d() highlight <- tibble(   region = c("precentral"),   highlight = c("#FF0000") )  ggsegray(   .data = highlight,   atlas = dk(),   hemisphere = "left",   colour_by = "highlight",   na_colour = "#FFFFFF",   material = list(lit = FALSE) ) |>   pan_camera("left lateral") |>   set_background("white")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-flat.png") rgl::close3d() ggsegray(   atlas = dk(),   hemisphere = "left",   material = list(front = "lines") ) |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::snapshot3d("img/ray-wireframe.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Everything works rgl scene produced ggsegray(). use rayshader rendering, others plain rgl.

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Depth field blurs regions away focal point, pulling attention specific structure. render_depth() applies post-processing step snapshot: focus (0-1) controls focal plane sits depth buffer. focallength fstop control strength blur — lower f-stop means shallower depth field, just like real camera lens.
code: ggsegray(   .data = some_data,   atlas = dk(),   colour_by = "p",   hemisphere = "left" ) |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rayshader::render_depth(   filename = "depth_of_field.png",   focus = 0.5,   focallength = 200,   fstop = 4,   width = 600,   height = 450 ) rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: Place text labels directly 3D scene rgl::text3d():
code: ggsegray(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::text3d(x = -45, y = 10, z = 55, text = "precentral", cex = 0.8)  rgl::snapshot3d("labelled.png") rgl::close3d()

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: rgl::movie3d() spins camera around scene writes frames GIF — handy conference talks supplementary materials: Adjust duration rpm control length speed. higher quality, use rayshader::render_movie() ray-traces frame:
code: ggsegray(   .data = some_data,   atlas = dk(),   colour_by = "p",   hemisphere = "left" ) |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  rgl::movie3d(   rgl::spin3d(axis = c(0, 1, 0), rpm = 10),   duration = 3,   dir = tempdir(),   movie = "brain_spin",   type = "gif",   clean = TRUE ) rgl::close3d() rayshader::render_movie(   filename = "brain_rotate.mp4",   frames = 360,   fps = 30,   zoom = 0.8,   phi = 30 )

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: multi-panel figures, render view separately stitch together magick:
code: library(magick)  views <- c("left lateral", "left medial", "right lateral", "right medial") files <- paste0("panel_", gsub(" ", "_", views), ".png")  for (i in seq_along(views)) {   hemi <- if (grepl("left", views[i])) "left" else "right"    ggsegray(     .data = some_data,     atlas = dk(),     colour_by = "p",     hemisphere = hemi   ) |>     pan_camera(views[i]) |>     set_background("white")    rayshader::render_highquality(     filename = files[i],     samples = 256,     width = 800,     height = 600   )    rgl::close3d() }  panels <- image_read(files) combined <- image_montage(panels, geometry = "800x600", tile = "2x2") image_write(combined, "figure_all_views.png")  file.remove(files)

title: Ray-traced brain renders with rayshader
text: typical publication workflow: Explore interactively ggseg3d() htmlwidget viewer Switch ggsegray() settled atlas, data, colour scheme Draft rgl::snapshot3d() iterate camera angle lighting Render render_highquality() final figure Combine panels magick needed

title: Using ggseg3d in Shiny
text: Use ggseg3dOutput() UI renderGgseg3d() server:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Brain Atlas Viewer"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput(         "hemi",         "Hemisphere",         choices = c("left", "right", "both"),         selected = "left"       )     ),     mainPanel(       ggseg3dOutput("brain", height = "500px")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$brain <- renderGgseg3d({     hemi <- if (input$hemi == "both") NULL else input$hemi      ggseg3d(hemisphere = hemi) |>       pan_camera("left lateral")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using ggseg3d in Shiny
text: Set explicit dimensions ggseg3dOutput(): use set_dimensions() render function:
code: ggseg3dOutput("brain", width = "100%", height = "600px") renderGgseg3d({   ggseg3d() |>     set_dimensions(width = 800, height = 600) })

title: Using ggseg3d in Shiny
text: Update brain plot data changes:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   brain_data <- reactive({     tibble(       region = c("precentral", "postcentral", "insula"),       value = runif(3)     )   })    output$brain <- renderGgseg3d({     ggseg3d(       .data = brain_data(),       atlas = dk(), # nolint [object_usage_linter]       colour_by = "value"     ) |>       pan_camera("left lateral")   }) }

title: Using ggseg3d in Shiny
text: Use updateGgseg3dCamera() updateGgseg3dBackground() modify existing widget without re-rendering: updates faster re-rendering entire widget.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput(         "view",         "View",         choices = c(           "left lateral",           "left medial",           "right lateral",           "right medial"         )       ),       selectInput("bg", "Background", choices = c("white", "black", "grey"))     ),     mainPanel(       ggseg3dOutput("brain")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$brain <- renderGgseg3d({     ggseg3d() |>       pan_camera("left lateral")   })    observeEvent(input$view, {     updateGgseg3dCamera("brain", input$view)   })    observeEvent(input$bg, {     updateGgseg3dBackground("brain", input$bg)   }) }

title: Using ggseg3d in Shiny
text: ’s full app data selection dynamic updates:
code: library(shiny) library(ggseg3d) library(dplyr)  example_data <- tibble(   region = c(     "precentral",     "postcentral",     "insula",     "superior parietal",     "inferior parietal",     "supramarginal",     "cuneus",     "pericalcarine"   ),   thickness = c(2.5, 2.3, 3.1, 2.2, 2.4, 2.6, 1.8, 1.9),   volume = c(8500, 7200, 6800, 9100, 8800, 7500, 4200, 3800) )  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Brain Metrics Explorer"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput("metric", "Metric", choices = c("thickness", "volume")),       selectInput(         "view",         "Camera View",         choices = c(           "left lateral",           "left medial",           "right lateral",           "right medial",           "left superior",           "left inferior"         )       ),       checkboxInput("edges", "Show edges", value = FALSE),       selectInput("bg", "Background", choices = c("white", "black", "grey90"))     ),     mainPanel(       ggseg3dOutput("brain", height = "600px")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$brain <- renderGgseg3d({     p <- ggseg3d(       .data = example_data,       atlas = dk(), # nolint [object_usage_linter]       colour_by = input$metric,       text_by = input$metric     ) |>       pan_camera(input$view) |>       set_background(input$bg)      if (input$edges) {       p <- p |> set_edges("black")     }      p   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using ggseg3d in Shiny
text: Minimize re-renders: Use updateGgseg3dCamera() updateGgseg3dBackground() camera background changes instead re-rendering. Debounce reactive data: data updates frequently, use debounce() avoid excessive re-renders. Pre-compute data: data transformations outside render function possible. Limit regions: Showing fewer regions renders faster. Filter atlas need specific structures.

title: Interactive 3D Brain Atlas Visualization
text: Interactive 3D brain atlas visualization R. Plot brain parcellations WebGL meshes powered Three.js, render publication-quality static images rgl rayshader. pipe-friendly API lets map data onto brain regions, control camera angles, toggle region edges, overlay glass brains, snapshot result.

title: Interactive 3D Brain Atlas Visualization
text: Install CRAN: get development version ggsegverse r-universe:
code: install.packages("ggseg3d") options(   repos = c(     ggsegverse = "https://ggsegverse.r-universe.dev",     CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"   ) ) install.packages("ggseg3d")

title: Interactive 3D Brain Atlas Visualization
text: Three atlases ship package (via ggseg.formats): dk – Desikan-Killiany cortical atlas aseg – Automatic subcortical segmentation tracula – White-matter tract atlas Additional atlases available ggsegverse r-universe.

title: Interactive 3D Brain Atlas Visualization
text: Subcortical structures translucent glass brain overlay:  See package website full walkthrough, rayshader rendering, Shiny integration.
code: ggseg3d(atlas = dk(), hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral") ggseg3d(atlas = aseg()) |>   add_glassbrain() |>   pan_camera("right lateral")

title: Interactive 3D Brain Atlas Visualization

what: Add glass brain to ggseg3d plot — add_glassbrain
title: Add glass brain to ggseg3d plot — add_glassbrain
text: Adds translucent brain surface ggseg3d plot anatomical reference. Particularly useful subcortical tract visualizations spatial context helps interpretation. Works htmlwidget (`ggseg3d`) rgl (`ggsegray`) objects.

title: Add glass brain to ggseg3d plot — add_glassbrain
code: add_glassbrain(   p,   hemisphere = c("left", "right"),   surface = "pial",   colour = "#CCCCCC",   opacity = 0.3,   brain_meshes = NULL )

title: Add glass brain to ggseg3d plot — add_glassbrain
text: p `ggseg3d` widget `ggsegray` rgl object. hemisphere Character vector. Hemispheres add: "left", "right", . surface Character. Surface type: "inflated", "white", "pial". colour Character. Colour glass brain surface (hex named). opacity Numeric. Transparency glass brain (0-1). brain_meshes Optional user-supplied brain meshes. See [ggseg.formats::get_brain_mesh()] format details.

title: Add glass brain to ggseg3d plot — add_glassbrain
text: input object (modified), piping.

title: Add glass brain to ggseg3d plot — add_glassbrain
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ggseg3d(atlas = aseg()) |>   add_glassbrain("left", opacity = 0.2)  ggsegray(atlas = aseg()) |>   add_glassbrain(opacity = 0.15) |>   pan_camera("right lateral") } # }

text: Processes colour mapping ggseg_atlas objects using vertex-based colouring.

what: Build mesh list for cortical atlases — build_cortical_meshes

title: ggseg3d: Plot brain segmentations in 3D — ggseg3d-package
text: package contains data various brain parcellations, convenient functions inspect results directly brain-plot using interactive 3D mesh visualizations powered Three.js via htmlwidgets. package uses `ggseg_atlas` objects ggseg.formats contain 3D vertex mappings.

title: ggseg3d: Plot brain segmentations in 3D — ggseg3d-package
text: Maintainer: Athanasia Mo Mowinckel .m.mowinckel@psykologi.uio.(ORCID) Authors: Didac Vidal-Piñeiro d.v.pineiro@psykologi.uio.(ORCID)

title: Shiny bindings for ggseg3d — ggseg3d-shiny
text: outputId output variable read width, height Must valid CSS unit (like '100%', '400px', 'auto') number, coerced string 'px' appended. expr expression generates ggseg3d env environment evaluate expr. quoted expr quoted expression (quote())? useful want save expression variable.

title: Plot 3D brain parcellations — ggseg3d
text: `ggseg3d` creates returns interactive Three.js brain mesh visualization. Dispatches atlas-type-specific methods via [prepare_brain_meshes()].

title: Plot 3D brain parcellations — ggseg3d
code: ggseg3d(   .data = NULL,   atlas = dk(),   label_by = "region",   text_by = NULL,   colour_by = "colour",   palette = NULL,   na_colour = "darkgrey",   na_alpha = 1,   ...,   label = deprecated(),   text = deprecated(),   colour = deprecated() )

title: Plot 3D brain parcellations — ggseg3d
text: .data data.frame use plot aesthetics. Must include column called "region" corresponding regions. atlas `ggseg_atlas` object containing 3D vertex mappings, string naming atlas function (deprecated). label_by String. Column name used hover label region. text_by String. Column name extra hover text shown region label. colour_by String. Column name mapped mesh colours. palette String. Vector colour names HEX colours. Can also named numeric vector, colours names, breakpoint colour value na_colour String. Either name, hex RGB colour NA colour. na_alpha Numeric. number 0 1 control transparency NA-regions. ... Type-specific arguments passed atlas method. See section **Type-specific arguments** . label, text, colour `r lifecycle::badge("deprecated")` Use `label_by`, `text_by`, `colour_by` instead.

title: Plot 3D brain parcellations — ggseg3d
text: htmlwidget object interactive 3D brain visualization

title: Plot 3D brain parcellations — ggseg3d
text: Cortical atlases (`cortical_atlas`): `surface` Surface type: `"LCBC"` (default, alias     inflated), `"inflated"`, `"semi-inflated"`, `"white"`, `"pial"`. `hemisphere` Character vector hemispheres: `"right"`,     `"left"`. `edge_by` Column name region boundary edges. `brain_meshes` Custom brain mesh data. Tract atlases (`tract_atlas`): `tract_color` `"palette"` (default) `"orientation"`     (direction-based RGB). `tube_radius` Tube radius (numeric, default 5). `tube_segments` Tube segment count (integer, default 8).

title: Plot 3D brain parcellations — ggseg3d
text: Athanasia Mowinckel Didac Piñeiro

title: Plot 3D brain parcellations — ggseg3d
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ggseg3d() ggseg3d(hemisphere = "left") |> pan_camera("left lateral") ggseg3d() |> set_legend(FALSE) ggseg3d() |> set_background("black") } # }

what: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
title: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
text: Creates rgl 3D scene brain atlas. Uses atlas preparation pipeline [ggseg3d()] outputs rgl instead htmlwidgets. resulting scene can piped [pan_camera()], [add_glassbrain()], [set_background()], rendered rayshader's `render_highquality()` captured `rgl::snapshot3d()`.

title: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
code: ggsegray(   .data = NULL,   atlas = dk(),   label_by = "region",   text_by = NULL,   colour_by = "colour",   palette = NULL,   na_colour = "darkgrey",   na_alpha = 1,   material = list(),   ...,   label = deprecated(),   text = deprecated(),   colour = deprecated() )

title: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
text: .data data.frame use plot aesthetics. Must include column called "region" corresponding regions. atlas `ggseg_atlas` object containing 3D vertex mappings, string naming atlas function (deprecated). label_by String. Column name used hover label region. text_by String. Column name extra hover text shown region label. colour_by String. Column name mapped mesh colours. palette String. Vector colour names HEX colours. Can also named numeric vector, colours names, breakpoint colour value na_colour String. Either name, hex RGB colour NA colour. na_alpha Numeric. number 0 1 control transparency NA-regions. material Named list rgl material properties passed [rgl::tmesh3d()]. Controls mesh surface shaded. ... Type-specific arguments passed atlas method. See section **Type-specific arguments** . label, text, colour `r lifecycle::badge("deprecated")` Use `label_by`, `text_by`, `colour_by` instead.

title: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
text: object class `ggsegray` (invisibly), wraps   rgl device ID. Pipe [pan_camera()], [add_glassbrain()],   [set_background()] modify scene.

title: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
text: Useful material list entries: `specular` `"black"` (matte) `"white"` (glossy). `shininess` Specular exponent. Higher = tighter highlights. `lit` `FALSE` disables lighting. `alpha` Transparency, 0 (invisible) 1 (opaque). `smooth` `TRUE` Gouraud shading, `FALSE` flat. See [rgl::material3d()] full list.

title: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
text: Cortical atlases (`cortical_atlas`): `surface` Surface type: `"LCBC"` (default, alias     inflated), `"inflated"`, `"semi-inflated"`, `"white"`, `"pial"`. `hemisphere` Character vector hemispheres: `"right"`,     `"left"`. `edge_by` Column name region boundary edges. `brain_meshes` Custom brain mesh data. Tract atlases (`tract_atlas`): `tract_color` `"palette"` (default) `"orientation"`     (direction-based RGB). `tube_radius` Tube radius (numeric, default 5). `tube_segments` Tube segment count (integer, default 8).

title: Render brain atlas with rgl — ggsegray
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ggsegray(hemisphere = "left") |>   pan_camera("left lateral")  ggsegray(atlas = aseg()) |>   add_glassbrain(opacity = 0.15) |>   pan_camera("right lateral") |>   set_background("black") } # }

what: Prepare brain meshes and legend data — prepare_brain_meshes

title: Prepare brain meshes and legend data — prepare_brain_meshes
code: prepare_brain_meshes(atlas, ...)  # S3 method for class 'cortical_atlas' prepare_brain_meshes(   atlas,   .data = NULL,   surface = "LCBC",   hemisphere = c("right", "left"),   label_by = "region",   text_by = NULL,   colour_by = "colour",   palette = NULL,   na_colour = "darkgrey",   na_alpha = 1,   edge_by = NULL,   brain_meshes = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'subcortical_atlas' prepare_brain_meshes(   atlas,   .data = NULL,   label_by = "region",   text_by = NULL,   colour_by = "colour",   palette = NULL,   na_colour = "darkgrey",   na_alpha = 1,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'tract_atlas' prepare_brain_meshes(   atlas,   .data = NULL,   label_by = "region",   text_by = NULL,   colour_by = "colour",   palette = NULL,   na_colour = "darkgrey",   na_alpha = 1,   tract_color = c("palette", "orientation"),   tube_radius = 2,   tube_segments = 10,   ... )

title: Prepare brain meshes and legend data — prepare_brain_meshes
text: atlas `ggseg_atlas` object ... Type-specific arguments passed methods .data Optional user data merge surface Surface type: `"inflated"` (default), `"semi-inflated"`, `"white"`, `"pial"`. Use `"LCBC"` alias `"inflated"`. hemisphere Character vector hemispheres: `"right"`, `"left"`. label_by Column name region hover labels text_by Column name extra hover text colour_by Column name colour values palette Colour palette specification na_colour Colour NA values na_alpha Transparency NA regions edge_by Column name region boundary edge grouping brain_meshes Optional user-supplied brain meshes tract_color `"palette"` (default) `"orientation"` (direction-based RGB colouring) tube_radius Numeric tube radius (default 5 `NULL`). tube_segments Integer tube segment count (default 8 `NULL`).

title: Set hemisphere positioning mode — set_positioning
text: p ggseg3d widget object positioning position hemispheres: - "anatomical": Offset medial surfaces adjacent midline.   Left negative x, right positive x. Best displaying   hemispheres together. - "centered": Center hemisphere origin. Best   single-hemisphere snapshots consistent sizing needed.

title: ggseg3d 2.0.0

title: ggseg3d 2.0.0
text: Complete rewrite using htmlwidgets Three.js instead plotly New pipe-friendly API chainable addition functions Support unified brain_atlas format ggseg.formats package

title: ggseg3d 2.0.0
text: set_background() - set background colour set_legend() - control legend visibility set_dimensions() - set widget width height set_edges() - add region boundary edges set_flat_shading() - disable lighting exact colours set_orthographic() - use orthographic projection pan_camera() - position camera standard anatomical views add_glassbrain() - add translucent brain surface overlay snapshot_brain() - save widget PNG image edge.parameter edge grouping data columns

title: ggseg3d 2.0.0
